seo.title: Legal RAG Pipeline AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for rag pipeline AI agents in legal. Covers Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules, Court filing standards.

content.intro: Deploying a RAG pipeline in legal contexts carries extraordinary risk: a single hallucinated case citation can result in court sanctions, malpractice liability, and bar discipline, as demonstrated by the Mata v. Avianca precedent. Legal RAG systems must enforce strict source fidelity, attorney oversight, and privilege protections at every layer of the retrieval-synthesis pipeline. This checklist provides concrete, enforceable controls tailored to the unique compliance demands of legal AI deployment.

sections.heading: Document Ingestion & Privilege Protection
sections.description: Controls to ensure that only properly classified, privilege-safe documents enter the vector store and that client confidential data is never exposed to model training pipelines.

items.label: Classify documents for attorney-client privilege before ingestion
items.description: Implement a pre-ingestion tagging step that flags documents as privileged, work-product, or public using metadata from your DMS. Block privileged documents from entering shared or multi-tenant vector indexes.

items.label: Enforce client matter isolation in vector namespaces
items.description: Partition your vector database by client matter ID so that retrieval queries for Matter A cannot surface documents from Matter B. Validate namespace isolation with automated cross-matter query tests before go-live.

items.label: Prohibit ingested client documents from being used in model fine-tuning
items.description: Add a contractual and technical control in your LLM provider agreement and pipeline configuration to ensure no document chunk sent as context is logged or used for downstream training. Audit provider data retention policies quarterly.

items.label: Implement document-level access control lists (ACLs) tied to user roles
items.description: Map each ingested document to the attorneys and staff authorized to access it. Enforce ACLs at query time so the retriever only returns chunks the authenticated user is permitted to see.

items.label: Log all document ingestion events with timestamps and source metadata
items.description: Maintain an immutable audit log of every document added to or removed from the vector store, including file hash, matter ID, ingestion timestamp, and the identity of the user who triggered ingestion.

items.label: Scan ingested documents for personally identifiable information (PII) before chunking
items.description: Run an automated PII detection pass on each document prior to embedding. Flag documents containing SSNs, financial account numbers, or medical data for manual review before they are chunked and indexed.

items.label: Establish a document freshness policy with automated expiry for superseded legal sources
items.description: Set TTL rules for case law, statutes, and regulations so that overruled cases or repealed statutes are automatically removed or flagged as stale in the index. Integrate with a legal research update feed (e.g., Westlaw KeyCite signals).

sections.heading: Retrieval Accuracy & Citation Verification
sections.description: Mechanisms to ensure that every case, statute, or regulation surfaced by the retriever is real, verifiable, and accurately represented in the generated output — directly addressing the Mata v. Avianca hallucination risk.

items.label: Validate every cited case against a canonical legal database before output delivery
items.description: After LLM synthesis, extract all citations from the generated text and programmatically verify each one against Westlaw, LexisNexis, or CourtListener APIs. Block output delivery if any citation returns a 'not found' or 'overruled' status.

items.label: Require source chunk attribution for every factual claim in generated output
items.description: Configure the LLM prompt to produce inline citations referencing the specific retrieved chunk (document ID, page, paragraph) for each factual assertion. Reject outputs that contain unsourced legal claims.

items.label: Implement a hallucination detection layer using entailment scoring
items.description: After generation, run an NLI (natural language inference) model to score whether each claim in the output is entailed by the retrieved context. Flag outputs with entailment scores below 0.85 for mandatory attorney review before use.

items.label: Set a minimum retrieval relevance threshold and reject low-confidence retrievals
items.description: Configure your vector search to return only chunks with a cosine similarity score above a defined threshold (e.g., 0.78). If insufficient high-confidence chunks are found, return a 'insufficient source material' response rather than allowing the LLM to speculate.

items.label: Test retrieval pipeline against a golden dataset of known legal Q&A pairs monthly
items.description: Maintain a curated evaluation set of 100+ legal questions with verified correct citations and answers. Run automated regression tests monthly to detect retrieval drift or embedding model degradation.

items.label: Display retrieved source documents alongside generated output in the attorney UI
items.description: Present the top-k retrieved chunks with their full source metadata (case name, citation, date, court) in a side panel so the reviewing attorney can directly verify the basis for each generated statement.

items.label: Flag and quarantine any output that cites a case with negative treatment (overruled, distinguished)
items.description: Integrate KeyCite or Shepard's signals into the post-generation citation check. Automatically append a warning label to any output referencing a case with negative appellate history and require attorney sign-off before the output is used.

sections.heading: Attorney Oversight & Human-in-the-Loop Controls
sections.description: Workflow controls that ensure a licensed attorney reviews, approves, and takes professional responsibility for all AI-generated legal content before it is used in client matters or filed with any court.

items.label: Require mandatory attorney review and digital sign-off before any output is used in a client matter
items.description: Implement a workflow gate that prevents AI-generated memos, briefs, or advice from being saved to a client file, emailed to a client, or filed with a court until a licensed attorney has reviewed and digitally approved the output with their bar number logged.

items.label: Display a persistent disclaimer on all AI-generated content stating it requires attorney review
items.description: Watermark or header-stamp every generated document with 'DRAFT — AI-GENERATED — REQUIRES LICENSED ATTORNEY REVIEW BEFORE USE' and ensure this label cannot be removed until the attorney approval workflow is completed.

items.label: Prohibit direct client-facing delivery of AI-generated legal advice without attorney intermediation
items.description: Configure the system to route all outputs through an attorney review queue. Block any API endpoint or UI flow that would allow AI-generated legal analysis to be delivered directly to a client without attorney review.

items.label: Log attorney review decisions with timestamps, reviewer identity, and any modifications made
items.description: Capture a complete audit trail of each attorney review event: who reviewed, when, what changes were made to the AI output, and the final approved version. Store logs for a minimum of 7 years to support malpractice defense.

items.label: Implement confidence scoring thresholds that escalate low-confidence outputs to senior review
items.description: Define confidence tiers based on hallucination scores, retrieval relevance, and citation validity. Outputs below a medium-confidence threshold must be escalated to a supervising attorney rather than a junior associate for review.

items.label: Provide attorneys with a structured feedback interface to flag incorrect outputs and retrain retrieval
items.description: Build a feedback UI where reviewing attorneys can mark specific claims as incorrect, cite the correct source, and submit corrections. Feed this data into a retrieval quality improvement loop, not LLM fine-tuning on client data.

items.label: Conduct quarterly attorney training on AI limitations, hallucination risks, and review responsibilities
items.description: Deliver documented training sessions covering the Mata v. Avianca case, common RAG failure modes, and each attorney's professional responsibility obligations when using AI tools. Track completion and store records for bar compliance purposes.

sections.heading: eDiscovery Compliance & Court Filing Standards
sections.description: Controls specific to eDiscovery workflows and court filing requirements, ensuring AI-assisted document review and brief generation meet federal and state court standards for accuracy, disclosure, and proportionality.

items.label: Disclose AI tool usage in court filings per applicable local rules and standing orders
items.description: Before filing any document assisted by AI generation, check the presiding court's standing orders and local rules for AI disclosure requirements (e.g., N.D. Tex., D. Colo., and others with explicit AI disclosure mandates). Include required disclosure language in the filing.

items.label: Maintain a defensible audit trail for all AI-assisted eDiscovery review decisions
items.description: For each document reviewed or coded with AI assistance, log the AI's suggested coding, the reviewer's final decision, and any override rationale. This log must be producible in response to a discovery challenge under FRCP Rule 26.

items.label: Validate that AI-assisted privilege review does not inadvertently waive privilege through over-production
items.description: Before producing any AI-reviewed document set, run a secondary privilege screen by a licensed attorney on a statistically significant sample. Document the sampling methodology and review rate to support a clawback motion under FRE 502(d) if needed.

items.label: Ensure RAG-generated deposition summaries and chronologies cite specific Bates numbers
items.description: Configure the RAG pipeline for eDiscovery summarization tasks to require Bates number attribution for every factual assertion in generated chronologies, deposition summaries, and hot document reports.

items.label: Test AI-assisted document review recall rate against a statistically valid control set before production
items.description: Before relying on AI-assisted review for a production set, validate recall using a random sample control review. Document that recall meets the agreed-upon or court-approved threshold (typically 75%+ recall for TAR workflows).

items.label: Implement chain-of-custody logging for all documents processed through the RAG pipeline in litigation matters
items.description: Record every transformation a litigation document undergoes in the pipeline: ingestion, chunking, embedding, retrieval, and inclusion in a generated output. This chain-of-custody log supports authentication of AI-assisted work product.

items.label: Verify that court-filed briefs generated with RAG assistance comply with word count and formatting rules
items.description: After AI-assisted drafting, run an automated compliance check against the applicable court's local rules for page limits, font, margin, and word count requirements before the attorney review and filing workflow is initiated.

items.label: Establish a litigation hold integration to prevent RAG pipeline from purging indexed documents subject to preservation obligations
items.description: Connect your vector store document lifecycle management to your litigation hold system. When a hold is placed on a matter, automatically suspend TTL expiry and deletion policies for all documents associated with that matter in the index.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: ABA Model Rule 1.1 (Competence) and Rule 5.3 (Supervision of Non-Lawyer Assistance) together require that attorneys using AI tools understand their limitations sufficiently to supervise outputs and maintain professional responsibility for all AI-generated work product — ignorance of how the RAG pipeline works is not a defense to a disciplinary complaint.

Following Mata v. Avianca (S.D.N.Y. 2023), multiple federal courts have issued standing orders requiring explicit disclosure of generative AI use in filings and certification by counsel that all citations have been verified against actual legal databases — your pipeline must support this certification workflow with a documented citation verification audit trail.

Under FRCP Rule 26(g) and the Sedona Principles, AI-assisted eDiscovery review processes must be documented and defensible, meaning your RAG pipeline's retrieval logic, relevance thresholds, and review workflows must be explainable to opposing counsel and courts upon challenge.

State bar ethics opinions (including those from NY, CA, FL, and TX) increasingly require that client confidential information shared with third-party AI vendors be governed by a written data processing agreement that prohibits training use, and that clients be informed when AI tools are used in their matters — your vendor contracts and client engagement letters must reflect these obligations.

cta.heading: Automate Your Legal Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your Legal RAG pipeline against this checklist, automatically flag citation failures and privilege risks, and generate audit-ready compliance reports for bar inquiries and court disclosure requirements.
